Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.

Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").

+++ b/scripts/capture_transcripts.py
@@ -73,7 +73,124 @@ def _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
     return re.compile(re.escape(name))
 
 
+def _display_name_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
+    """Redactions for the operator's full DISPLAY NAME (#J-002).
+
+    The username/email rules leave the real name (e.g. a git ``user.name`` like "[REDACTED]")
+    in cleartext — distinct operator PII that appears in git author strings, ``pyproject``
+    ``authors=``, and prose, often right beside an already-redacted email. Derive candidate names
+    from ``git config user.name`` and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES`` env (comma-separated), redact each
+    (and, for a multi-word name, its whitespace-flexible form). Length-guarded to avoid
+    over-redacting common words.
+    """
+    names: list[str] = []
+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_NAMES", "")
+    names.extend(n.strip() for n in extra.split(",") if n.strip())
+    # Derive the operator name from SEVERAL sources — any one may be empty in a given context
+    # (#J-002 follow-up): on this node local `git config user.name` is unset yet commits still
+    # carry an author via global config / env, so a single-source lookup scrubbed nothing. Cover
+    # the env author vars, git config, AND the actual last-commit author.
+    for env_name in ("GIT_AUTHOR_NAME", "GIT_COMMITTER_NAME"):
+        v = os.environ.get(env_name, "").strip()
+        if v:
+            names.append(v)
+    try:
+        import subprocess
+
+        for cmd in (["git", "config", "user.name"], ["git", "log", "-1", "--format=%an"]):
+            out = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
+            if out.returncode == 0 and out.stdout.strip():
+                names.append(out.stdout.strip())
+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
+        pass
+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
+    seen: set[str] = set()
+    for n in names:
+        if len(n) < 4 or n in seen:
+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
+        seen.add(n)
+        # Flexible inter-token whitespace (e.g. "[REDACTED]"). NO leading \b: the name often
+        # appears glued to a JSON-escaped newline ("...---\\n[REDACTED]") where \b doesn't fire
+        # because the escape's 'n' abuts the name (#J-002 follow-up). A trailing \b still prevents
+        # matching inside a longer word (e.g. "Joneses"); the >=4-char guard bounds over-redaction.
+        # CASE-INSENSITIVE (#J-002 confirm-concern round 2): the operator's name also appears
+        # lowercased as actor/handle tokens — "[REDACTED]@hcgrc" (an initiated_by field), "[REDACTED]-[REDACTED]
+        # (human)" in actor tables — which a case-sensitive pattern misses. re.IGNORECASE covers all
+        # casings; "Joneses"/"joneses" stays protected by the trailing \b either way.
+        toks = n.split()
+        pats.append(re.compile(r"\s+".join(re.escape(t) for t in toks) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
+        # Redact each NAME TOKEN standalone too (#J-002 confirm-concern): the full-name pattern
+        # leaves the operator's bare first/last name in cleartext — e.g. "[REDACTED] approves ..." and
+        # "[REDACTED] builds and governs ..." appear in governance prose hundreds of times, uniquely
+        # re-identifying the operator. Per-token redaction (len>=4, trailing \b, no leading \b for
+        # the JSON-\n-glued case) closes it. Over-redaction is bounded: the tokens come only from
+        # the verified operator name and the corpus contains no unrelated bearer of these tokens
+        # (all 256 "[REDACTED]" contexts were sampled — every one is an operator governance reference).
+        for t in toks:
+            if len(t) >= 4 and t.lower() not in seen:
+                seen.add(t.lower())
+                pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(t) + r"\b", re.IGNORECASE))
+    return pats
+
+
+def _repo_handle_patterns() -> list[re.Pattern[str]]:
+    """Redactions for the operator's repo owner / GitHub handle (#J-008).
+
+    The handle (the ``<owner>`` in ``github.com/<owner>/<repo>``, e.g. in PR/issue URLs and
+    ``gh --repo <owner>/...`` commands) is operator-identifying PII that NO existing rule covers:
+    it is not an email (the email rule needs an ``@``), and it differs from the home-dir username
+    the bare-username rule derives. Left uncovered it leaked across hundreds of transcript files
+    (#J-008). Derive candidate handles from the git remotes and the ``ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES`` env;
+    redact each as a bare token (length-guarded). A handle is a single unambiguous string, so no
+    word-boundary is needed — redact it inside URLs, ``gh`` args, and the ``<handle>@`` email stem.
+    Matched case-insensitively, and the distinctive STEM (handle minus a generic ``the`` prefix) is
+    also redacted, because the handle leaks via derived names — e.g. ``[REDACTED]-audience-profiles``
+    (a skill name) embeds the handle minus ``the`` (#J-008 confirm-concern).
+    """
+    handles: list[str] = []
+    extra = os.environ.get("ASSAY_SCRUB_HANDLES", "")
+    handles.extend(h.strip() for h in extra.split(",") if h.strip())
+    try:
+        import subprocess
+
+        out = subprocess.run(["git", "remote", "-v"], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)
+        if out.returncode == 0:
+            for m in re.finditer(r"github\.com[:/]([^/\s]+)/", out.stdout):
+                handles.append(m.group(1))
+    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - git absent/misconfigured must never break capture
+        pass
+    # Expand each handle with its distinctive stem (drop a leading generic "the") so handle-derived
+    # names/slugs are covered too (#J-008 confirm-concern).
+    expanded: list[str] = []
+    for h in handles:
+        expanded.append(h)
+        if h.lower().startswith("the") and len(h) - 3 >= 4:
+            expanded.append(h[3:])
+    pats: list[re.Pattern[str]] = []
+    seen: set[str] = set()
+    for h in expanded:
+        key = h.lower()
+        if len(h) < 4 or key in seen:
+            continue  # too short → risks over-redacting common words; or already added
+        seen.add(key)
+        if len(h) >= 8:
+            # SEPARATOR-FLEXIBLE (#J-008 confirm-concern round 3): a GitHub handle is usually a
+            # de-spaced brand ("[REDACTED]" = "[REDACTED]"); the SPACED source brand
+            # appears in prose/links and lets a reader reconstruct the redacted handle/email by
+            # concatenation. Allow punctuation/whitespace between each character so the spaced,
+            # hyphenated, and dotted forms all redact — while the de-spaced form (empty separators)
+            # still matches. The separator class is space/._- ONLY, so unrelated prose cannot
+            # accidentally spell out the handle, and common single tokens (e.g. "CISO") are NOT
+            # redacted because the WHOLE letter sequence (…[REDACTED]) must be present in order.
+            pats.append(re.compile(r"[\s._-]*".join(re.escape(c) for c in h), re.IGNORECASE))
+        else:
+            pats.append(re.compile(re.escape(h), re.IGNORECASE))
+    return pats
+

53	        """An MlflowClient bound to the local store. Uses the client API (keyed by run_id)
54	        throughout, so no run ever sits on the global active-run stack — start_run followed by
55	        log_metric cannot collide (audit issue #O1)."""
56	        try:
57	            from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient
58	        except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised only without the extra
59	            raise RuntimeError(

61	            ) from exc
62	        return MlflowClient(tracking_uri=self._uri)
63	
64	    def _experiment_id(self, client: Any) -> str:
65	        exp = client.get_experiment_by_name(self._experiment)
66	        if exp is not None:

68	        # check-then-create is a TOCTOU (#J-004): two concurrent first-time start_run calls (the
69	        # 8-thread reasoning pool, or multiple study processes on one shared local store) can both
70	        # see None and both create_experiment; MLflow rejects the loser with RESOURCE_ALREADY_EXISTS
71	        # and the exception would propagate out of start_run, leaving run_id=None and silently
72	        # dropping ALL metrics + the provenance artifact for the whole study. On a create race,
73	        # re-fetch the now-existing experiment instead of failing.
74	        try:

88	        # accumulate over time (pass 3, #F-023). Terminate it FAILED on any param-logging error
89	        # before re-raising, so a created run is never left dangling.
90	        try:

"""License gate (#147): fail if any installed dependency carries a strong-copyleft license.

Reads a pip-licenses JSON report (path as argv[1], default ``licenses.json``) and exits non-zero
if any package's license matches a denied strong-copyleft family that would be incompatible with
the Apache-2.0 distribution. LGPL (weak copyleft, dynamic linking) is permitted. Advisory
monitoring of the rest is handled by Dependabot (.github/dependabot.yml).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import sys

# Strong-copyleft families denied in the distributed dependency set. Both the SPDX-style short
# tokens AND the full prose names must be listed (pass 3, #F-015): a package reporting
# "Server Side Public License" or "European Union Public Licence 1.2" contains neither the "SSPL"
# nor "EUPL" token, so the short tokens alone let those families bypass the gate.
_DENY = (
    "GPL",
    "AGPL",
    "SSPL",
    "EUPL",
    "SERVER SIDE PUBLIC",  # SSPL prose form
    "EUROPEAN UNION PUBLIC",  # EUPL prose form
    "AFFERO",  # AGPL prose form ("GNU Affero General Public License")
)


def main(path: str = "licenses.json") -> int:
    with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:  # context manager — no leaked fd (#F-050)
        packages = json.load(f)
    bad = []
    unknown = []
    for pkg in packages:
        raw = (pkg.get("License") or "").strip()
        lic = raw.upper()
        # A package whose license is empty / UNKNOWN / unspecified could be hiding a copyleft the
        # deny-substring scan can't see. Don't fail on it (pip-licenses reports UNKNOWN for many
        # legitimate packages with incomplete metadata), but SURFACE it as a CI warning so a
        # reviewer checks it instead of it passing silently (#H-007).
        if not raw or lic in ("UNKNOWN", "UNKNOWN LICENSE", "NONE"):
            unknown.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}")
            continue
        if "LGPL" in lic:  # weak copyleft — permitted for dynamically-linked libraries
            continue
        if any(tok in lic for tok in _DENY):
            bad.append(f"{pkg.get('Name')} {pkg.get('Version')}: {pkg.get('License')}")
    if unknown:
        print(
            "::warning::license gate: "
            + str(len(unknown))
            + " package(s) report no/UNKNOWN license — review for hidden copyleft: "
            + ", ".join(sorted(unknown))
        )
    if bad:
        print("::error::strong-copyleft licenses found:\n" + "\n".join(bad))
        return 1
    print(f"license gate OK ({len(packages)} packages checked, {len(unknown)} unknown)")
    return 0

findings.title: J-001 fix silently corrupts NUL-free binary transcript artifacts (provenance integrity break)
findings.claim: The J-001 binary classifier copies a file verbatim ONLY when a NUL byte is present (`if b"\x00" in raw`); otherwise it does `raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace").encode("utf-8")`. Any genuinely binary file that contains no 0x00 byte (e.g. a JPEG/image fragment, a latin-1/Windows-1252 subprocess dump, a gzip member) is therefore lossily rewritten: every invalid byte becomes U+FFFD (0xEF 0xBF 0xBD), changing both content and length. Verified end-to-end: `_copy_scrubbed` on `b"\xff\xd8\xff\xe0binarydata\xc2\xa9\xff"` produced `b"\xef\xbf\xbd...binarydata\xc2\xa9\xef\xbf\xbd"`. The pre-J-001 code preserved such files verbatim via the UnicodeDecodeError branch, so this is a NEW regression introduced by the J-001 fix.
findings.why_real: main() captures the session subtree with `rglob("*")` (line 274), i.e. ALL files, not just .jsonl/.json — so any binary artifact in the subtree is in scope. The manifest's sha256 (line 251) is computed over the corrupted `data`, so the committed transcript is silently mangled while the manifest looks internally consistent. This directly defeats the stated 'transcripts are committed as provenance' guarantee for NUL-free binary files: the committed bytes no longer equal the source bytes, with no error and no signal.
findings.suggested_fix: Restore lossless binary preservation: classify binary by NUL byte AND by UTF-8 decodability, copying verbatim if either indicates binary, while still scrubbing decodable text. E.g. `try: text = raw.decode("utf-8")` and scrub on success; on UnicodeDecodeError, copy verbatim; treat NUL as binary. The fail-closed goal (always scrub anything that is text) is preserved because a file that decodes cleanly as UTF-8 is text and gets scrubbed, while undecodable bytes are preserved rather than mangled. If errors='replace' must be kept for partial-text JSONL, gate the verbatim path on `b"\x00" in raw OR not utf8-decodable` so true binaries are never re-encoded.

findings.title: Binary-verbatim test is non-discriminating: fixture contains a NUL byte, so it cannot catch the K-P7-1 corruption regression
findings.claim: This test's stated intent is 'a genuinely binary file (undecodable) is copied unchanged, not corrupted', but its fixture is `b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"` which CONTAINS `\x00`. It therefore exercises only the NUL-present verbatim branch and passes regardless of the K-P7-1 bug. No test exercises a NUL-free binary file, which is exactly the corruption case. The test gives false confidence that binary preservation works.
findings.why_real: Pass-7's own discipline (it caught J-001 as a regression and added fail-closed tests) is undermined here: the regression test that should protect the 'copied unchanged, not corrupted' invariant is structurally blind to the failure mode, because removing the `\x00` from the fixture would flip it to FAILING. A passing test that cannot fail on the bug it claims to guard is a coverage gap, not coverage.
findings.suggested_fix: Add (or change the fixture to) a NUL-FREE binary blob, e.g. `b"\xff\xd8\xff\xe0\xc2\xa9\xff"`, and assert `dst.read_bytes() == src.read_bytes()`. With the current K-P7-1 code this assertion fails (proving the bug); after the K-P7-1 fix it passes. Keep the NUL-present case as a second assertion.

findings.title: J-003 build-lock staleness gate will false-fail on every PR when a time-versioned build dep releases
findings.claim: The new gate regenerates requirements-build.lock from `printf 'hatchling\neditables\n'` and diffs against the committed lock, which pins time-versioned transitive deps such as `trove-classifiers==2026.6.1.19` and `packaging==26.2` (see requirements-build.lock). Unlike the surrounding `requirements.lock`/`requirements-core.lock` regen, these build inputs are unconstrained by any pyproject range, so when a new trove-classifiers/packaging release appears (trove-classifiers ships roughly weekly), `uv pip compile` resolves the newer version and the diff fails — failing the build-lock gate on PRs that did not touch the lockfile.
findings.why_real: This is the exact failure class the same job already calls out and works around for the main locks ('transient resolver/index differences ... that fail this gate on every PR even when the lockfiles are current'). The build-lock branch was added without the same mitigation, so it reintroduces a known spurious-CI-failure mode. It is a maintenance/availability defect (blocks merges), not a correctness one, hence low.
findings.suggested_fix: Either pin the build inputs the same way the resolver is pinned (uv version already pinned) and add a tolerance/header-stripping note, or constrain the build deps (e.g. pin trove-classifiers / packaging in the .in file), or scope the gate to fail only when hatchling/editables direct pins change. At minimum document the regen command so the false-fail is a fast fix rather than a mystery.

Pass-7 fix-regression audit complete. I diffed pass-7's own changes (commits `dbb7d5c..37a4597`) across all four target files and verified each fix empirically.

**Findings:**

- **K-P7-1 (high)** — The J-001 NUL-byte binary classifier in `scripts/capture_transcripts.py:240-243` introduced a follow-on regression: a genuinely binary file with NO NUL byte (image fragment, latin-1 dump, gzip member) is now silently corrupted via `decode(errors="replace").encode()` — every invalid byte becomes U+FFFD. Verified end-to-end through `_copy_scrubbed`. Since `main()` captures the whole subtree via `rglob("*")` and the manifest sha256 is over the corrupted bytes, this defeats the "committed as provenance" guarantee with no error. The pre-J-001 code preserved these verbatim.

- **K-P7-2 (medium)** — `tests/test_scripts.py:117` `test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim` is non-discriminating: its fixture `b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"` contains a `\x00`, so it only exercises the NUL-present branch and passes regardless of K-P7-1. The exact corruption case (NUL-free binary) is untested.

- **K-P7-3 (low)** — The J-003 build-lock staleness gate (`ci.yml:143-147`) will false-fail on every PR when a time-versioned build dep (`trove-classifiers`, `packaging`) releases — the same spurious-CI-failure class the surrounding main-lock gates explicitly work around, but the build-lock branch was added without that mitigation.

**Verified sound (no defect):** the MLflow TOCTOU re-fetch (`tracking.py:74-80`) — broad except is bounded, single re-fetch (no infinite state), re-raises original on genuine failure; the `_assert_transient` helper — correctly discriminates transient from RateLimit/Permanent subclasses, making previously non-discriminating tests meaningful; `--no-build-isolation` — hatchling+editables+transitive deps are all in `requirements-build.lock` with versions consistent across all three locks, so the editable build resolves; J-006 `--with-system` removal — correct scoping (and build-lock deps are now installed in-env, so coverage is not reduced).
